# Callout

The callout component is a highlighted content block designed to emphasize important information. It supports single or multiple callouts within a container, with options for different themes, alignments, and optional background images. Callouts are effective for drawing attention to key programs, services, or announcements.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the callout's properties in real-time. Test different themes, alignments, link types, and background images.

<Canvas of={CalloutStories.Callout} />
<Controls of={CalloutStories.Callout} />

## Properties

### Optional Properties

{twigPropsTable(componentProps, 'optional')}

## Component Variations

### Single vs. Multiple Callouts

The callout component automatically adjusts its layout based on the number of callouts provided:

- **Single Callout**: Displays as a full-width highlighted block
- **Two Callouts**: Displays side-by-side in a two-column layout
- **Multiple Callouts**: Automatically wraps into a responsive grid

### Alignment Options

- **Center**: Text and elements are center-aligned (default)
- **Left**: Text and elements are left-aligned for a more traditional reading flow

### Link Types

- **Button**: Displays the link as a prominent button for primary actions
- **Link**: Displays as a text link for secondary actions or when a lighter touch is needed

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-callout.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-callout.scss`

### Accessibility

- Semantic HTML structure with proper heading hierarchy
- Links and buttons are keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
- Color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards
- Background images use overlays to ensure text readability
- Supports screen readers with proper ARIA attributes

### Responsive Behavior

- Single callouts display full-width on all screen sizes
- Multiple callouts stack vertically on mobile devices
- Two-column layout appears on tablet and larger screens
- Background images are responsive and optimized for performance

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use callouts to highlight 2-3 key programs, services, or initiatives
- Keep heading text concise (3-5 words)
- Limit body text to 1-2 sentences for scannability
- Choose alignment based on content density (center for short content, left for longer text)
- Use button links for primary actions, text links for secondary actions
- Select theme dials that provide sufficient contrast with surrounding content
- When using background images, ensure they don't interfere with text readability

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use more than 3-4 callouts in a single container (creates visual clutter)
- Don't use callouts for lengthy content (use text fields or other components instead)
- Avoid background images with busy patterns that reduce text legibility
- Don't mix different alignment styles within the same callout group
- Don't use callouts for critical navigation (use menus or site structure instead)
- Avoid using callouts on every page (reduces their impact)

### When to Use Callouts

- Highlighting degree programs or academic offerings
- Featuring key services or resources
- Promoting upcoming events or deadlines
- Drawing attention to important initiatives
- Creating visual entry points to different site sections

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all theme variations and configurations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-callout-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Text Field**: Use for longer-form content that doesn't need visual emphasis
- **Call to Action (CTA)**: Consider standalone CTAs for single, prominent actions
- **Card**: Use cards when you need more structured content with images
